refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with end-to-end communication latency that ensures safe remote operation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is mostly atomic, focusing on teleoperation latency and its compatibility with safety. The phrase 'compatible with safe remote operation' is kept to emphasize the safety aspect without specifying how latency is achieved or measured, maintaining solution neutrality. The FR does not contain multiple obligations requiring splitting.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the end-to-end communication latency during teleoperation under agreed network conditions and verify that it meets the safety criteria defined by the safety manager and teleoperation standards (e.g., p95 latency ≤ 150 ms).
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with end-to-end communication latency that ensures safe remote operation.

candidate_dps: Implement a communication protocol optimized for low-latency end-to-end data transfer
Design a system architecture that prioritizes real-time data processing and minimal buffering
Incorporate latency monitoring and adaptive control mechanisms to maintain latency within threshold
Separate teleoperation control commands from non-critical data streams to reduce communication load
Use predictive control algorithms to compensate for communication delays
selected_dp.selected_dp: Design a system architecture that prioritizes real-time data processing and minimal buffering

potential_sources: Tight coupling between communication protocol and control logic
Shared resources causing delays in data processing
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP focuses on architectural design to minimize latency and supports independence by separating concerns. Potential coupling risks arise if communication and control are not modularized properly.

assumptions_risks: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters to meet latency requirements
Operational policies do not introduce additional delays or constraints
Latency measurement and monitoring are accurate and timely
Predictive control algorithms may be needed if latency approaches threshold
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including a DP for latency monitoring and adaptive control to dynamically maintain latency within limits under varying network conditions.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the core system architecture responsible for real-time data processing and minimal buffering, integrating necessary components and managing data flow efficiently.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Bi-directional data stream interface for real-time telemetry data exchange.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Network communication interface enabling data transfer to and from external systems.

module_containment_scores.notes: Highly cohesive as NGHE Solution is the main system architecture module; minimal diffusion as real-time processing is central here.
Partial containment possible but real-time processing logic would diffuse into other modules; Vehicle focuses more on physical control.
Teleoperation involves remote control but real-time data processing is broader than teleoperation scope.
Safety Services focus on alerts and safety logic, not core real-time data processing.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most specific and appropriate boundary to contain the DP of real-time data processing with minimal buffering, as it centralizes the system architecture and data flow management, minimizing diffusion across other modules.
containment_risks: Potential interface complexity with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules requiring careful interface design to avoid unintended diffusion.
Real-time constraints may require close coordination with Safety Services, risking some diffusion if safety logic tightly couples with data processing.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP logically addresses the FR by focusing on real-time data processing and minimal buffering, which directly supports the requirement for end-to-end communication latency ensuring safe remote operation.

diffusion_risks: Potential interface complexity with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules requiring careful interface design to avoid unintended diffusion.
Real-time constraints may require close coordination with Safety Services, risking some diffusion if safety logic tightly couples with data processing.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module is highly cohesive and appropriately contains the DP, centralizing real-time data processing and minimizing diffusion. Other modules have lower containment scores and higher diffusion risks, supporting the choice of NGHE Solution as the primary IM boundary.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible through measuring telemetry latency (p95 ≤ 150 ms) under agreed network conditions, which can be monitored via the NGHE Solution's interfaces.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the DP to the NGHE Solution IM is clear and well justified, with explicit interfaces defined for telemetry and network link supporting the functional requirement.

recommended_revisions: Explicitly document interface contracts between NGHE Solution and Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules to manage diffusion risks.
Include monitoring and logging capabilities within NGHE Solution to verify latency requirements in operation.
Consider defining submodules or components within NGHE Solution to further isolate real-time processing functions and reduce complexity.

decision.justification: All gates have passed, indicating that the current DP and IM adequately address the FR with feasible verification and clear allocation. However, the recommended revisions suggest further decomposition within the NGHE Solution IM to isolate real-time processing functions and reduce complexity, which is critical given the high safety criticality and diffusion risks identified. Therefore, deriving next-level sub-FRs to enable detailed decomposition and refinement is appropriate to enhance modularity, manage interface complexity, and support rigorous verification.
next_level_sub_frs: Ensure real-time telemetry data processing with minimal latency
Manage interface contracts with Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules
Implement monitoring and logging for latency verification
Isolate submodules within NGHE Solution for real-time processing functions

revise.why: To explicitly define submodules or components within the NGHE Solution IM to reduce complexity and manage diffusion risks, improving containment and verification capabilities.

assumptions: The NGHE Solution module can be further decomposed into meaningful submodules.
Interface contracts can be clearly specified and enforced.
Monitoring and logging capabilities can be integrated without violating real-time constraints.
open_risks: Potential interface complexity leading to unintended diffusion.
Tight coupling between safety logic and data processing risking diffusion.
Verification challenges if monitoring is insufficient or intrusive.
evidence_needed: Detailed interface specifications between NGHE Solution and other modules.
Design of submodules within NGHE Solution with clear responsibilities.
Verification plans demonstrating latency measurement and monitoring effectiveness.
